headline: 6 Corporate Groups Control 85% of Malaysian Media Market
context: Press freedom analysis found 6 politically-affiliated groups control 85% of media market across print broadcast and digital.

cards.big: 6 politically-linked groups control 85% of Malaysian media market
cards.sub: Concentration undermines editorial independence and information diversity

cards.big: Media Prima linked to UMNO controls 4 TV channels 3 newspapers 12 digital
cards.sub: Combined reach of 18 million Malaysians makes it dominant in news

cards.big: Star Media and Sin Chew controlled by MCA-linked entities own 28% print
cards.sub: Chinese-language media concentrated in 2 groups with aligned editorial positions

cards.big: Only 4 independent digital outlets reach more than 500,000 monthly readers
cards.sub: Malaysiakini and FMT face constant legal and financial pressure

cards.big: Media concentration tests whether information diversity can survive ownership consolidation
6 groups control 85% of media. Ownership concentration makes editorial independence structurally impossible.
